signals.headline: **Context on Ceasefire/Blockade**: A prior 2-week US-Iran ceasefire (announced ~April 8) appears fragile, with ongoing m
signals.raw: **Context on Ceasefire/Blockade**: A prior 2-week US-Iran ceasefire (announced ~April 8) appears fragile, with ongoing mine-clearing, no direct US Navy attacks, but Iranian drone strikes on Gulf allies like Bahrain; blockade allows non-Iranian transit but fails to fully halt Iranian ships, prompting strategy recalibration[1][2][3].
matched_markets: Will the US x Iran ceasefire be extended by April 21, 2026?
Trump announces US x Iran ceasefire end by April 21, 2026?

signals.headline: South Korea Inflation Rate Rises to 2
signals.raw:
South Korea Inflation Rate Rises to 2.2% in March 2026, Below Forecasts but Above BoK Target Amid Oil Shocks | NO | HIGH confidence** [1][4]

South Korea's annual inflation rate increased to 2.2% in March 2026 from 2.0% in prior months, missing market expectations of 2.4% while exceeding the Bank of Korea's 2% target.[1] This data, released around early April 2026, aligns with the BoK's April 10 d

signals.headline: **Key Development**: In a New York Post interview reported today (April 15, 2026), President Trump stated negotiations w
signals.raw: **Key Development**: In a New York Post interview reported today (April 15, 2026), President Trump stated negotiations with Iran could resume within the next 2 days, potentially in Islamabad, marking a diplomatic pivot after Day 1 reports of limited success in the US naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz launched April 13[2][3].

signals.headline: **Prediction Market Impact**: This shift from military pressure to talks could move markets on US-Iran de-escalation, oi
signals.raw: **Prediction Market Impact**: This shift from military pressure to talks could move markets on US-Iran de-escalation, oil prices, or conflict resolution bets, though conflicting reports (e.g., slow/uncertain talks vs. imminent resumption) temper certainty[2].
